elements.sent-2411-001: In this paper, we present new necessary conditions and sufficient conditions for the (positive parts of) the varieties of vertically parametrized systems to admit monomial parametrizations.
elements.sent-2411-002: The conditions are based on a combination of polyhedral geometry and previously known results about injectivity of monomial maps.
elements.sent-2411-003: The motivation arises from the study of steady state varieties of reaction networks, as toricity simplifies the determination of multistationarity substantially.
elements.sent-2411-004: Toric varieties are central objects in combinatorial algebraic geometry, and appear naturally in many applications, including equation solving \cite{Telen2022 , statistics \cite{GeigerMeekSturmfels2006 , phylogenetics \cite{SturmfelsSullivant2005 , and chemical reaction network theory \cite{Craciun2009toric .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-005: Much is known about the geometry of toric varieties (see, e.g., \cite{Cox2011toric for an overview), but effectively deciding if a given variety is toric from an implicit description remains a hard problem, that has recently been approached from the point of view of, e.g., symbolic computation \cite{Grigoriev2020toricity and Lie theory \cite{maraj2023symmetry,kahle2024efficiently .}}}
elements.sent-2411-006: In this paper, we study the following parametric variation of the problem: Given a parametrized system $F\in\RR[\k_1,\ldots,\k_m,x_1^\pm,\ldots,x_n^\pm]$ (with parameters $\k$ and variables $x$), when are the solution sets over $\GG\in\{\RR_{>0 ,\RR^*,\CC^*\}$ toric in the sense that there exists an exponent matrix $A\in\ZZ^{d\times n}$ such that for all choices of parameters $\k\in\GG^m$, the set of zeros $\VV_{\GG (F_k)\subseteq\GG^n$ is either empty or admits a monomial parametrization \begin{equation}\GG^d\to\GG^n,\quad t\mapsto \alpha_\k\circ t^A\end{equation} for some $\alpha_\k\in\GG^n$ depending on the parameters $\k$.}}
elements.sent-2411-007: In this case, we write $\VV_{\GG (F_k)=\alpha_\k\circ\T_A^{\GG}$ where $\T_A^{\GG =\{t^A:t\in\GG^d\}$, and we view $\VV_{\GG (F_k)$ as a coset of the multiplicative group $\T_A^{\GG}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-008: In \cite{Rahkooy2021parametric , the authors show that this can be formulated as a quantifier elimination problem, which in principle gives an algorithm for solving the problem over $\RR^*$ and $\RR_{>0}$, albeit at a substantial computational cost.}
elements.sent-2411-009: Another general but computationally intense approach for $\GG=\RR_{>0}$ is taken in \cite{sadeghimanesh2019grobner,rahkooy2021comprehensive which give sufficient conditions in terms of Gröbner bases and comprehensive Gröbner systems.}
elements.sent-2411-010: Here, we focus on the case when $F$ is \term{vertically parametrized in the language of \cite{Helminck2022generic,feliu2023dimension , in the sense that it can be written on the form \begin{equation}F=C(\k\circ x^M)\in\RR[\k_1,\ldots,\k_m,x_1^\pm,\ldots,x_n^\pm]^s\end{equation} where each row of $C\in\RR^{s\times m}$ encodes a linear combination of $m$ monomials with exponents given by the columns of $M\in\ZZ^{n\times m}$, scaled by the parameters $\k=(\k_1,\ldots,\k_m)$ through component-wise multiplicaiton $\circ$.}}
elements.sent-2411-011: For example, with \begin{equation}C= 0 2 -1 1 -1 2  \quadand\quad M= 0 1 2 1 2 3 ,\end{equation} we obtain the polynomial system \begin{equation}F= ( \kappa_2\, x_1 x_2^2 - \kappa_3\, x_1^2x_2^3 , \kappa_1\, x_2 - \kappa_2\, x_1 x_2^2 +2 \kappa_3\, x_1^2x_2^3 ) \, .\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-040: We consider vertically parametrized systems (or vertical systems for short), which by definition are parametric systems of the form \begin{equation}\label{eq:NkB} F=C(a\circ x^M)\in \kk[\k_1,\ldots,\k_m,x_1^\pm,\ldots,x_n^\pm]^s,\end{equation} consisting of $s\leq n$ polynomials with parameters $\k=(\kappa_1,\ldots,\kappa_m)$ and variables $x=(x_1,\ldots,x_n)$, encoded by a coefficient matrix $ C\in \kk^{s\times m}$ and an exponent matrix $M \in \ZZ^{n\times m}$.
elements.sent-2411-041: The component-wise product $\k\circ x^M$ indicates that the monomial encoded by the $i$th column of $M$ is scaled by $\k_i$, while the rows of $C$ give linear combinations of the scaled monomials.
elements.sent-2411-042: An important feature is that the system $F$ is linear in the parameters and that each parameter accompanies always the same monomial (though a monomial can be accompanied by different parameters; this is achieved by considering repeated columns in $M$).

elements.algorithm-2411-001: [Toric invariance]\label{alg:invariance}\leavevmode \fontsize{10}{13}\selectfont \begin{algorithmic}[1] \REQUIRE{Matrix $C\in \kk^{s\times m}$ of full rank $s$, matrix $M\in\mathbb{Z}^{n\times m}$ } \ENSURE{Rank $d$ matrix $A\in \ZZ^{d\times n}$ such that the vertical system $F=C(\k\circ x^M)$ is $\T_A$-invariant over $\GG$. } \STATE{Find a basis $\{E_1,\dots,E_{m-s}\}$ for $\ker(C)$ consisting of elementary vectors} \IF{Union of supports of $E_1,\dots,E_{m-s}$ $\neq [m]$} \RETURN{``The varieties $\VV_{\GG}(F_\k)$ are empty for all $\k\in \GG^m$'' \# \it (It means $\ker(C) \cap \GG^m=\varnothing$)} \ENDIF \IF{$\GG=\RR_{>0}$ and (Interior of polyhedral cone $\ker(C)\cap\RR^m_{\geq 0}$ is empty)} \RETURN{``The varieties $\VV_{>0}(F_\k)$ are empty for all $\k\in \RR_{>0}^m$''} \ENDIF \STATE{\# \it We find the fundamental partition} \STATE{Find the fundamental partition $\{\rho_1,\dots,\rho_{\theta}\}$ from the supports of $\{E_1,\dots,E_{m-s}\}$} \STATE{For each $i \in [\theta]$, pick $j_0\in \rho_i$, and construct a matrix $B$ whose columns are $M_{j}-M_{j_0}$ for all $j \in \rho_{i}\setminus \{j_0\}$} \IF{$\GG=\RR_{>0}$ or $\CC^*$} \STATE{Find a $\ZZ$-matrix $A$ with rows a basis for $\ker_\QQ(B^\top)$} \ELSE \STATE{Find a $\ZZ$-matrix $A$ with rows a basis for $\ker_\ZZ(B^\top)$} \ENDIF \RETURN{$A$} \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-002: [Summary for $\RR_{>0}$]\label{alg:summary}\leavevmode \fontsize{10}{13}\selectfont \begin{algorithmic}[1] \REQUIRE{Matrices $C\in \RR^{s\times m}$ of rank $s$, $M\in\mathbb{Z}^{n\times m}$} \ENSURE{Whether $F=C(\k\circ x^M)$ is generically consistent, (generically) locally $\T_A$-toric, or (generically) $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \STATE{Run \Cref{alg:invariance}. Proceed if a maximal-rank matrix $A\in \ZZ^{d\times n}$ is returned (hence $\ker(C)\cap \RR^m_{>0}\neq \varnothing$} \STATE{\# \it Decide generic consistency} \STATE{Generate a random $w\in \ker(C)$ and compute $r:=\rk(C \diag(w) M^\top)$} \IF{$r<s$ and $\rk(C \diag(w) M^\top)<s$ for all $w\in \ker(C)$} \RETURN{``$F$ is not generically consistent over $\RR_{>0}$''} \ENDIF \IF{$s+d<n$} \RETURN{$F$ is not generically locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \STATE{\# \it At this point we know $F$ is generically locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ and proceed to study toricity and number of cosets} \STATE{\# \it Check injectivity by means of \Cref{thm:injectivity}} \IF{All coefficients of $\det(\mathcal{L}_{\mu,\alpha})$ have the same sign} \RETURN{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \STATE{Find $mv:=$ mixed volume of the coset counting system} \IF{$\rk(C \diag(w) M^\top)=s$ for all $w\in \ker(C)\cap \RR_{>0}^m$} \IF{$mv=1$} \RETURN{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \IF{Interior of polyhedral cone $\row(A)\cap\RR^n_{\geq 0}$ is nonempty and \Cref{prop:constant_number_of_cosets}(ii) holds} \STATE{Set $r:=$ number of solutions in $\RR^n_{>0}$ of the coset counting system for a random choice of $\k\in \RR^m_{>0}$} \IF{$r=1$} \RETURN{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \RETURN{$F$ is locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ with $r$ cosets} \ENDIF \RETURN{$F$ is locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ with constant number of cosets and at most $mv$} \ENDIF \IF{$mv=1$} \RETURN{$F$ is generically $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \RETURN{$F$ is generically locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ with at most $mv$ cosets} \end{algorithmic}
elements.sent-2411-409: The following algorithm, which will be relevant in \Cref{sec:crnt_perspectives , checks \Cref{prop:multistationarity and \Cref{prop:acr for systems that are generically locally toric.}}}
elements.algorithm-2411-003: [Multiple zeros and constant coordinates] \label{alg:applications}\leavevmode \fontsize{10}{13}\selectfont \begin{algorithmic}[1] \REQUIRE{Matrices $C\in \RR^{s\times m}$ of rank $s$, $M\in\mathbb{Z}^{n\times m}$, $L\in \RR^{(n-s)\times n}$} \ENSURE{Whether $(C(\k\circ x^M),Lx-b)$ admits multiple zeros in $\RR^n_{>0}$ and whether the zero set of $F=C(\k\circ x^M)$ has constant coordinates } \STATE{Run \Cref{alg:summary}. Proceed if a maximal-rank matrix $A\in \ZZ^{d\times n}$ is returned and $F$ is generically locally $\T_A$-toric} \STATE{\# \it Check constant coordinates} \FOR{$i\in [n]$} \IF{$A_i=0_d$} \IF{$F$ is locally $\T_A$-toric} \PRINT{For all $\k$, $\VV_{>0}(F_\k)$ is contained in a finite union of translates of $\{x_i=0\}$ } \IF{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric} \PRINT{there is one such hyperplane} \ENDIF \ENDIF \ELSE \PRINT{$\VV_{>0}(F_\k)$ is not contained in a finite union of translates of $\{x_i=0\}$ for all $\k$} \ENDIF \ENDFOR \STATE{\# \it Check multiple positive solutions} \IF{$\det(\Gamma_{\alpha})$ has coefficients of different sign} \PRINT{$(C(\k\circ x^M),Lx-b)$ admits multiple zeros in $\RR^n_{>0}$} \ELSIF{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric} \PRINT{$(C(\k\circ x^M),Lx-b)$ does not admit multiple zeros in $\RR^n_{>0}$} \ELSE \PRINT{It remains undecided whether $(C(\k\circ x^M),Lx-b)$ admits multiple zeros in $\RR^n_{>0}$} \ENDIF \end{algorithmic}

elements.sent-2411-414: Our main motivation for studying systems of the form \eqref{eq:NkB comes from chemical reaction network theory, which we now give a quick introduction to.}
elements.sent-2411-415: For a more thorough introduction, we refer the reader to \cite{Dickenstein2016invitation,Feinberg2019foundations .}
elements.sent-2411-416: A \term{reaction network on an ordered set $\S=\{X_1,\ldots,X_n\}$ of species is a collection of $m$ reactions between formal nonnegative linear combinations of the species (called complexes): \begin{equation}\label{eq:reaction_network}\sum_{i=1}^n \alpha_{ij} X_i \longrightarrow \sum_{i=1}^n \beta_{ij} X_i\,,\qquad j\in [m]\,,\end{equation} where $\alpha_{ij ,\beta_{ij}\in\ZZ_{\geq 0}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-417: The net production of the species in each of the reactions is encoded by the stoichiometric matrix $N=(\beta_{ij -\alpha_{ij )\in\ZZ^{n\times m}$.}}
elements.sent-2411-418: The concentration of the respective species is denoted by a vector $x=(x_1,\ldots,x_n)$ of nonnegative real numbers.
elements.sent-2411-419: Under common assumptions, these concentrations vary according to an autonomous ordinary differential equation system of the form \begin{equation}\label{eq:ODE} \tfrac{dx}{dt}=N(\k\circ x^M)\,,\end{equation} where $M \in \ZZ^{n\times m}$ is called the kinetic matrix and $\k=(\kappa_1,\ldots,\kappa_m)\in\RR^m_{>0}$ is a vector of rate constants, which typically are viewed as unknown parameters.
elements.sent-2411-420: The main example of this construction arises under the mass-action assumption, where $M=(\alpha_{ij )$ is the reactant matrix consisting of the coefficients of the left-hand sides of the reactions.}
elements.sent-2411-421: In this case, $\RR^n_{\geq 0}$ is forward-invariant by the ODE system \eqref{eq:ODE .}
elements.sent-2411-422: Letting $s=\rk(N)$, we choose a matrix $C\in \RR^{s\times n}$ of rank $S$ such that $\ker(C)=\ker(N)$.
elements.sent-2411-423: Then, the steady states of \eqref{eq:ODE are the zeros of the vertically parameterized system \begin{equation}F = C(\k\circ x^M)\in\RR[\k,x^\pm].\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-424: We call any such system \term{the steady state system (note that a choice of $C$ is implicitly made).}
elements.sent-2411-425: One is particularly interested in steady states where all concentrations are strictly positive.
elements.sent-2411-426: As trajectories of \eqref{eq:ODE are confined in parallel translates of $\im(N)$, by letting $L\in \RR^{(n-s)\times n}$ be any matrix whose rows form a basis for the left-kernel of $N$, trajectories are confined in the sets of the form \begin{equation}\{x\in\RR^n_{\geq 0}:Lx- b=0\},\qquad b\in \RR^{n-s},\end{equation} which are called \term{stoichiometric compatibility classes .}}
elements.sent-2411-427: Positive steady states in stoichiometric compatibility classes are then the positive zeros of the augmented vertically parametrized system \begin{equation}\label{eq:augmented_crn} (C(\k\circ x^M), Lx-b)\in\RR[\k,b,x^\pm].\end{equation}.

elements.sent-2411-571: In this paper, we study the following notion of \term{parametric toricity over $\GG\in\{\RR_{>0 ,\RR^*,\CC^*\}$: We say that a parametrized system $F\in\RR[\k_1,\ldots,\k_m,x_1^\pm,\ldots,x_n^\pm]^s$ (with parameters $\k$ and variables $x$) displays toricity over $\GG$ if there is an exponent matrix $A\in\ZZ^{d\times n}$ such that all nonempty zero sets $\VV_{\GG (F_\k)\subseteq\GG^n$ for $\k\in\GG^m$ admit a monomial parametrization of the form \begin{equation}\GG^d\to\GG^n,\quad t\mapsto \alpha_\k\circ t^A\end{equation} with $\alpha_\k\in\GG^n$ depending on $\k$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-572: In this case, we write $\VV_{\GG (F_\k)=\alpha_\k\circ\T_A^{\GG}$ where $\T_A^{\GG =\{t^A:t\in\GG^d\}$, and we view $\VV_{\GG (F_\k)$ as a coset of the multiplicative group $\T_A^{\GG}$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-573: Detecting this type of parametric toricity can formulated as a quantifier elimination problem \cite{Rahkooy2021parametric , which gives an algorithm for solving the problem over $\RR^*$ and $\RR_{>0}$, albeit at a substantial computational cost.}
elements.sent-2411-574: Here, we focus on the case when $F$ is \term{vertically parametrized in the language of \cite{Helminck2022generic,feliu2023dimension , in the sense that it can be written in the form \begin{equation}F=C(\k\circ x^M)\in\RR[\k_1,\ldots,\k_m,x_1^\pm,\ldots,x_n^\pm]^s\end{equation} where each row of $C\in\RR^{s\times m}$ encodes a linear combination of $m$ monomials with exponents given by the columns of $M\in\ZZ^{n\times m}$, scaled by the parameters $\k=(\k_1,\ldots,\k_m)$ through component-wise multiplication $\circ$.}}

elements.algorithm-2411-004: [Toric invariance] \leavevmode \fontsize{10}{13}\selectfont \begin{algorithmic}[1] \REQUIRE{Matrix $C\in \kk^{s\times m}$ of full rank $s$, matrix $M\in\mathbb{Z}^{n\times m}$ } \ENSURE{Rank $d$ matrix $A\in \ZZ^{d\times n}$ such that $F=C(\k\circ x^M)$ is $\T_A$-invariant over $\GG$. } \STATE{Find a basis $\{E_1,\dots,E_{m-s}\}$ for $\ker(C)$ consisting of elementary vectors} \IF{(Union of supports of $E_1,\dots,E_{m-s}$) $\neq [m]$} \RETURN{``The varieties $\VV_{\GG}(F_\k)$ are empty for all $\k\in \GG^m$''} \ENDIF \IF{$\GG=\RR_{>0}$ and (Interior of polyhedral cone $\ker(C)\cap\RR^m_{\geq 0}$ is empty)} \RETURN{``The varieties $\VV_{>0}(F_\k)$ are empty for all $\k\in \RR_{>0}^m$''} \ENDIF \STATE{Find the fundamental partition $\{\rho_1,\dots,\rho_{\theta}\}$ from the supports of $\{E_1,\dots,E_{m-s}\}$} \STATE{For each $i \in [\theta]$, pick $j_0\in \rho_i$, and construct a matrix $B$ with columns $M_{j}-M_{j_0}$ for all $j \in \rho_{i}\setminus \{j_0\}$} \IF{$\GG=\RR_{>0}$ or $\CC^*$} \STATE{Find a $\ZZ$-matrix $A$ with rows a basis for $\ker_\QQ(B^\top)$} \ELSE \STATE{Find a $\ZZ$-matrix $A$ with rows a basis for $\ker_\ZZ(B^\top)$} \ENDIF \RETURN{$A$} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:invariance}

Proceed if a maximal-rank matrix $A\in \ZZ^{d\times n}$ is returned (hence $\ker(C)\cap \RR^m_{>0}\neq \varnothing$} \STATE{\# \it Decide generic consistency} \STATE{Generate a random $w\in \ker(C)$ and compute $r:=\rk(C \diag(w) M^\top)$} \IF{$r<s$ and $\rk(C \diag(w) M^\top)<s$ for all $w\in \ker(C)$} \RETURN{``$F$ is not generically consistent over $\RR_{>0}$''} \ENDIF \IF{$s+d<n$} \RETURN{$F$ is not generically locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \STATE{\# \it At this point we know $F$ is generically locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ \# \it We proceed to study toricity and number of cosets} \label{line:injectity_test} \IF{All coefficients of $\det(\mathcal{L}_{\mu,\alpha})$ have the same sign} \RETURN{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \STATE{Find $mv:=$ mixed volume of the coset counting system} \IF{$\rk(C \diag(w) M^\top)=s$ for all $w\in \ker(C)\cap \RR_{>0}^m$} \IF{$mv=1$} \RETURN{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \IF{Interior of polyhedral cone $\row(A)\cap\RR^n_{\geq 0}$ is nonempty and \Cref{prop:constant_number_of_cosets}(ii) holds} \STATE{Set $r:=$ number of solutions in $\RR^n_{>0}$ of the coset counting system for a random choice of $\k\in \RR^m_{>0}$} \IF{$r=1$} \RETURN{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \RETURN{$F$ is locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ with $r$ cosets} \ENDIF \RETURN{$F$ is locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ with constant number of cosets and at most $mv$} \ENDIF \IF{$mv=1$} \RETURN{$F$ is generically $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \RETURN{$F$ is generically locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ with at most $mv$ cosets} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:summary}

elements.sent-2411-685: We consider the problem of deciding whether the complex, real or positive solution sets of a parametrized polynomial system are toric (in the sense that they admit a monomial parametrization) for either all parameter values or generically in parameter space.
elements.sent-2411-686: We focus on vertically parametrized systems, which are sparse systems where we allow linear dependencies between coefficients in front of the same monomial.
elements.sent-2411-687: We give a matroid-theoretic characterization of the maximal-dimensional torus for which all solution sets are invariant under componentwise multiplication.
elements.sent-2411-688: Building on this, we provide necessary conditions and sufficient conditions for when the solution sets are unions of finitely many or a unique coset.
elements.sent-2411-689: Our results are applied to steady state systems of reaction networks, where toricity substantially simplifies the determination of multistationarity, and we illustrate our criteria by scanning for toricity in a database of biological models.
elements.sent-2411-690: Toric varieties are central objects in combinatorial algebraic geometry, and appear naturally in many applications, including equation solving \cite{sottile:book,Telen2022 , statistics \cite{GeigerMeekSturmfels2006 , phylogenetics \cite{SturmfelsSullivant2005 , and chemical reaction network theory \cite{Craciun2009toric .}}}}
elements.sent-2411-691: Much is known about the geometry of toric varieties (see, e.g., \cite{Cox2011toric for an overview), but effectively deciding whether a given variety is toric from an implicit description remains a hard problem, that has recently been approached from the point of view of, e.g., symbolic computation \cite{Grigoriev2020toricity and Lie theory \cite{maraj2023symmetry,kahle2024efficiently .}}}
elements.sent-2411-692: In this paper, we study the following notion of parametric toricity over $\GG\in\{\RR_{>0 ,\RR^*,\CC^*\}$: We say that a parametrized system $F\in\RR[\k_1,\ldots,\k_m,x_1^\pm,\ldots,x_n^\pm]^s$ (with parameters $\k$ and variables $x$) displays toricity over $\GG$ if there is an exponent matrix $A\in\ZZ^{d\times n}$ such that all nonempty zero sets $\VV_{\GG (F_\k)\subseteq\GG^n$ for $\k\in\GG^m$ admit a monomial parametrization of the form \begin{equation}\GG^d\to\VV_{\GG}(F_\k),\quad t\mapsto \alpha_\k\circ t^A\end{equation} with $\alpha_\k\in\GG^n$ depending on $\k$, and $\circ$ denoting componentwise multiplication.}}
elements.sent-2411-693: In this case, we view $\VV_{\GG (F_\k)$ as a coset of the multiplicative subgroup $\T_A^{\GG =\{t^A:t\in\GG^d\}\subseteq\GG^n$, and write \begin{equation}\VV_{\GG}(F_\k)=\alpha_\k\circ\T_A^{\GG}.\end{equation}.}}
elements.sent-2411-694: Detecting this type of parametric toricity can be formulated as a quantifier elimination problem \cite{Rahkooy2021parametric , which gives an algorithm for solving the problem over $\GG\in\{\RR^*,\RR_{>0}\}$, albeit at a substantial computational cost.}
elements.sent-2411-695: Another general but computationally intense approach for $\GG=\RR_{>0}$ is taken in \cite{sadeghimanesh2019grobner,rahkooy2021comprehensive , which give sufficient conditions in terms of Gröbner bases and comprehensive Gröbner systems.}
elements.sent-2411-696: Here, we focus on the special case when $F$ is \term{vertically parametrized in the language of \cite{Helminck2022generic,FeliuHenrikssonPascual2025vertical , in the sense that it can be written as \begin{equation}F=C(\k\circ x^M)\in\RR[\k_1,\ldots,\k_m,x_1^\pm,\ldots,x_n^\pm]^s\end{equation} where each row of $C\in\RR^{s\times m}$ encodes a linear combination of $m$ monomials with exponents given by the columns of $M\in\ZZ^{n\times m}$, scaled by the parameters $\k=(\k_1,\ldots,\k_m)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-697: Vertically parametrized systems describe the steady states of reaction networks, but also, e.g., the critical points of multivariate polynomials.

elements.algorithm-2411-006: [Toric invariance] \leavevmode \fontsize{10}{13}\selectfont \begin{algorithmic}[1] \REQUIRE{Matrix $C\in \kk^{s\times m}$ of full rank $s$, matrix $M\in\mathbb{Z}^{n\times m}$ } \ENSURE{Rank $d$ matrix $A\in \ZZ^{d\times n}$ such that $F=C(\k\circ x^M)$ is $\T_A$-invariant over $\GG$. } \STATE{Find a basis $\{E_1,\dots,E_{m-s}\}$ for $\ker(C)$ consisting of circuit vectors} \IF{(Union of supports of $E_1,\dots,E_{m-s}$) $\neq [m]$} \RETURN{``$\VV_{\GG}(F_\k)=\varnothing$ for all $\k\in \GG^m$''} \ENDIF \IF{$\GG=\RR_{>0}$ and (Interior of polyhedral cone $\ker(C)\cap\RR^m_{\geq 0}$ is empty)} \RETURN{``$\VV_{>0}(F_\k)=\varnothing$ for all $\k\in \RR_{>0}^m$''} \ENDIF \STATE{Find the matroid partition $\{\rho_1,\dots,\rho_{\theta}\}$ from the supports of $\{E_1,\dots,E_{m-s}\}$} \STATE{Construct $\widehat{M}\in \ZZ^{(n+\theta)\times m}$ from \Cref{rk:Cayley}} \IF{$\GG=\RR_{>0}$ or $\CC^*$} \STATE{Find a $\ZZ$-matrix $\widehat{A}$ with rows a basis for $\ker_\QQ(\widehat{M}^\top)$} \ELSE \STATE{Find a $\ZZ$-matrix $\widehat{A}$ with rows a basis for $\ker_\ZZ(\widehat{M}^\top)$} \ENDIF \RETURN{$A=$ first $n$ columns of $\widehat{A}$} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:invariance}

elements.algorithm-2411-007: [{Summary for $\RR_{>0}$}] \leavevmode \fontsize{10}{13}\selectfont \begin{algorithmic}[1] \REQUIRE{Matrices $C\in \RR^{s\times m}$ of rank $s$, $M\in\mathbb{Z}^{n\times m}$} \ENSURE{Whether $F=C(\k\circ x^M)$ is nondegenerate, (generically) locally $\T_A$-toric, or (generically) $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \STATE{Run \Cref{alg:invariance}. Proceed if a maximal-rank matrix $A\in \ZZ^{d\times n}$ is returned (hence $\ker(C)\cap \RR^m_{>0}\neq \varnothing$)} \STATE{\# \it Decide nondegeneracy} \STATE{Generate a random $w\in \ker(C)$ and compute $r:=\rk(C \diag(w) M^\top)$} \IF{$r<s$ and $\rk(C \diag(w) M^\top)<s$ for all $w\in \ker(C)$} \RETURN{``$F$ is not nondegenerate over $\RR_{>0}$''} \ENDIF \IF{$s+d<n$} \RETURN{$F$ is not generically locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \STATE{\# \it At this point we know $F$ is generically locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ \# \it We proceed to study toricity and number of cosets} \label{line:injectity_test} \IF{All coefficients of $\det(\mathcal{L}_{\mu,\alpha})$ have the same sign} \RETURN{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \STATE{Find $\mathit{mv}:=$ mixed volume of the coset counting system} \IF{$\rk(C \diag(w) M^\top)=s$ for all $w\in \ker(C)\cap \RR_{>0}^m$} \IF{$mv=1$} \RETURN{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \IF{Interior of $\row(A)\cap\RR^n_{\geq 0}$ is nonempty and condition \ref{it:no_boundary_zeros} in \Cref{prop:constant_number_of_cosets} holds} \STATE{Set $r:=$ number of solutions in $\RR^n_{>0}$ of the coset counting system for a random choice of $\k\in \RR^m_{>0}$} \IF{$r=1$} \RETURN{$F$ is $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \RETURN{$F$ is locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ with $r$ cosets} \ENDIF \RETURN{$F$ is locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ with constant number of cosets and at most $\mathit{mv}$} \ENDIF \IF{$\mathit{mv}=1$} \RETURN{$F$ is generically $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$} \ENDIF \RETURN{$F$ is generically locally $\T_A$-toric over $\RR_{>0}$ with generically $\mathit{mv}$ cosets} \end{algorithmic} \label{alg:summary}
